2XClient.Title: Acquired by Parallels and discontinued; continues as Parallels Client
2XClient.Notice: 2X Client was acquired by Parallels and discontinued under that name; it continues as Parallels Client. The version available here is no longer maintained and receives no security updates.

aMSNPortable.Title: Discontinued; ships an outdated, unpatched network or web engine
aMSNPortable.Notice: This application is discontinued and no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network or web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches, so any use that connects to the internet carries unpatched-vulnerability risk.

AudacityPortable.Title: Telemetry and vendor update check added after the 2021 acquisition
AudacityPortable.Notice: After Audacity was acquired by Muse Group in 2021, a revised privacy policy introduced limited telemetry (IP address, operating system, CPU type) and an update check that contacts the vendor, and referenced occasional data sharing with offices in Russia. This is a behavioral change from the original offline-only tool; it is not malware.

balenaEtcherPortable.Title: Collects usage analytics and contacts the vendor by default
balenaEtcherPortable.Notice: balenaEtcher collects usage analytics and contacts the vendor by default. This is not malicious behavior, but the application phones home unless analytics are disabled.

BitcoinPortable.Title: Full-node wallet; outdated versions are a financial risk
BitcoinPortable.Notice: This is a full-node cryptocurrency wallet. It is not malicious, but running an outdated version with real funds is a financial risk, since older node and wallet releases have shipped security and consensus-related fixes. Run the latest release and keep an offline backup of the wallet and keys.

CamStudioPortable.Title: Abandoned; official installer repeatedly flagged for adware and trojans
CamStudioPortable.Notice: CamStudio is abandoned, and its official installer was repeatedly flagged for bundled adware and trojan detections. Portable packaging excludes the official installer.

ccPortable.Title: Piriform product owned by Avast/Gen Digital; 2017 supply-chain compromise; default telemetry
ccPortable.Notice: This is a Piriform product, owned by Avast/Gen Digital. CCleaner, the vendor's flagship product, had its official build compromised in a 2017 supply-chain attack that distributed malware to roughly 2.27 million users, with a further intrusion attempt in 2019. Recent Piriform builds enable telemetry by default and include upsell prompts.

ConsolePortable.Title: Discontinued; succeeded by the ConsoleZ fork
ConsolePortable.Notice: Console2 is discontinued and no longer maintained. The active fork ConsoleZ exists as a replacement, though it is not offered here.

DetectItEasyPortable.Title: Legitimate reverse-engineering tool; possible antivirus false-positive
DetectItEasyPortable.Notice: This is a legitimate reverse-engineering or resource-editing tool. Because such tools are also used by malware authors, antivirus software may raise a false-positive. It is safe to use.

DiffpdfPortable.Title: Free version discontinued; application continues as a commercial product
DiffpdfPortable.Notice: The open-source version of diffpdf has been discontinued. The application continues under commercial licensing only.

DVDStylerPortable.Title: Official installer bundled adware during its SourceForge era
DVDStylerPortable.Notice: DVDStyler's official installer bundled adware during its SourceForge era. Portable packaging excludes the official installer. The application is otherwise legitimate but only lightly maintained.

EvincePortable.Title: Windows build discontinued; upstream continues on Linux only
EvincePortable.Notice: The independent Windows build of Evince has been discontinued. The upstream project remains active on Linux, but the version available here (2.32.0) dates from 2014.

FalkonPortable.Title: Discontinued Qt browser; ships an outdated, unpatched web-rendering engine
FalkonPortable.Notice: Falkon is discontinued and no longer maintained. It ships an outdated web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches, so any browsing carries unpatched-vulnerability risk.

FileZillaPortable.Title: Client is clean; official installer bundled adware for years
FileZillaPortable.Notice: The FileZilla client itself is clean and open source, but its official installer bundled third-party adware offers for years. Portable packaging excludes the official installer.

FotografixPortable.Title: Free version frozen; successor is no longer freeware
FotografixPortable.Notice: The Fotografix Classic free version (1.x) has been frozen. Fotografix 2026 and later are no longer available as freeware.

FreeDownloadManagerPortable.Title: Vendor distribution channel was compromised (2020 to 2022)
FreeDownloadManagerPortable.Notice: Free Download Manager's official website was compromised between 2020 and 2022 and selectively served a backdoored Linux build containing an information-stealer, confirmed by Kaspersky and later by the vendor. Windows builds were not affected, but the incident demonstrates that the vendor's distribution channel has been compromised in the past. Where a package retrieves files from the vendor at install time, its integrity depends on that channel; verify the download hash.

FreeFileSyncPortable.Title: Severely outdated; current release is 14.9
FreeFileSyncPortable.Notice: The version available here (6.2, from 2014) is severely outdated. FreeFileSync is actively maintained and currently at version 14.9.

FreeUPXPortable.Title: Executable packer; packed files often trip antivirus heuristics
FreeUPXPortable.Notice: FUPX is a legitimate executable compressor based on UPX. Packed executables frequently trigger antivirus heuristics, so the tool and the files it produces may be flagged as a false-positive.

fscPortable.Title: Became commercial from version 5.4; this is the last freeware release
fscPortable.Notice: FastStone Capture became a commercial product from version 5.4 onwards. The version available here (5.3) is the last release that was distributed as freeware.

GnumericPortable.Title: Windows build outdated; upstream development is slow
GnumericPortable.Notice: The Gnumeric Windows build available here (1.12.17, from 2014) is outdated. Upstream development continues slowly, but Windows builds have not kept pace.

HMNISEditPortable.Title: Discontinued since ~2005; no support for NSIS 3
HMNISEditPortable.Notice: HM NIS Edit has been discontinued since approximately 2005 and does not support NSIS 3. It is no longer a viable editor for modern NSIS scripts.

IcoFXPortable.Title: Became commercial from version 2.0; this is the last freeware release
IcoFXPortable.Notice: IcoFX became a commercial product from version 2.0 onwards. The version available here (1.6.4) is the last release that was distributed as freeware.

ImgBurnPortable.Title: Abandoned; official installer historically bundled adware
ImgBurnPortable.Notice: ImgBurn is no longer maintained; its last release was in 2013. Its official installer historically bundled adware (OpenCandy). Portable packaging excludes the official installer.

InstantbirdPortable.Title: Discontinued Mozilla-based instant messaging client
InstantbirdPortable.Notice: Instantbird is discontinued and no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

IObitUninstallerPortable.Title: Vendor known for aggressive bundling and upselling
IObitUninstallerPortable.Notice: IObit products have a long-standing reputation for aggressive bundling and upselling, and the company was accused in 2009 of using a competitor's detection database. The tools are functional, but vendor practices are a concern.

IronPortable.Title: Unofficial Chromium fork with overstated privacy claims
IronPortable.Notice: SRWare Iron is an unofficial Chromium fork. It renders all web traffic, depends on a small team for security updates, and has long been criticized for overstating its privacy benefits over standard Chromium. No malicious behavior is documented; the consideration is trust and patch cadence.

JkDefragPortable.Title: Discontinued; successor MyDefrag is also dead
JkDefragPortable.Notice: JkDefrag is discontinued and no longer maintained. Its successor MyDefrag has also been abandoned, leaving no active continuation of the project.

KasperskyTDSSKillerPortable.Title: Russia-based vendor; discontinued and subject to government warnings and bans
KasperskyTDSSKillerPortable.Notice: This is a Kaspersky tool. In 2022 Germany's Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) warned against using Kaspersky products, and in 2024 the United States prohibited their sale and updates over concerns about potential data access by Russian authorities. The tool is also discontinued and no longer receives updates.

KeePassProPortable.Title: KeePass 2.x had CVE-2023-32784 (fixed in 2.54); use the latest version
KeePassProPortable.Notice: KeePass 2.x ('Professional') was affected by CVE-2023-32784, which allowed recovery of the master password from a memory dump. It was fixed in version 2.54; ensure version 2.54 or later is in use. KeePass Classic (1.x) and KeePassXC were not affected.

KompoZerPortable.Title: Discontinued; ships an outdated, unpatched web-rendering engine
KompoZerPortable.Notice: KompoZer is a Mozilla-based WYSIWYG web editor discontinued around 2010. It ships an outdated web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches. Its intended successor BlueGriffon has also been abandoned.

MaxthonPortable.Title: Transmits browsing data to servers in China
MaxthonPortable.Notice: Maxthon is developed in China. Independent security researchers (Fidelis Cybersecurity and Exatel) documented that it transmits sensitive data — including the full browsing history, search queries, operating system details and a list of installed applications — to a server in Beijing, and that this continued even when the User Experience Improvement Program was disabled. Reporting from 2024 to 2025 indicates data is still sent to servers in China. This is browser-level behavior and is not affected by portable packaging.

MediaInfoPortable.Title: App is clean; only some official GUI installers offered adware
MediaInfoPortable.Notice: MediaInfo itself is clean and open source. Only some official GUI installer builds offered bundled adware; the command-line core and portable builds are unaffected.

MirandaPortable.Title: Discontinued; ships an outdated, unpatched network engine
MirandaPortable.Notice: Miranda IM is discontinued and no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

Notepad2Portable.Title: Discontinued; succeeded by Notepad3
Notepad2Portable.Notice: Notepad2 and its mod fork are both discontinued and no longer maintained. Notepad3 is the active successor and a recommended replacement.

OperaMailPortable.Title: Discontinued Opera email client; ships an outdated, unpatched network engine
OperaMailPortable.Notice: Opera Mail is discontinued and no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

OperaPortable.Title: China-based ownership; predatory loan-app scandal; browser telemetry
OperaPortable.Notice: Opera has been owned by a China-based consortium (majority Kunlun Tech) since 2016. In 2020, investigative reporting documented that the company operated predatory mobile loan apps that harvested users' contacts and charged extreme interest rates. The desktop browser also includes telemetry.

OperaPortableLegacy12.Title: Discontinued Presto-era Opera; no security updates
OperaPortableLegacy12.Notice: This is a legacy Opera build using the discontinued Presto engine, which receives no security updates. It is usable for compatibility or testing, but not for general or sensitive web browsing.

PDF-XChangeViewerPortable.Title: Discontinued; succeeded by PDF-XChange Editor
PDF-XChangeViewerPortable.Notice: PDF-XChange Viewer is discontinued and no longer receives security updates. It has been superseded by PDF-XChange Editor. As an unpatched PDF renderer, it may be vulnerable to malicious documents.

PhotoFilmStripPortable.Title: License changed in 3.1.2; free version line has been frozen
PhotoFilmStripPortable.Notice: PhotoFilmStrip changed its license from version 3.1.2 onwards. The free version line has been frozen and receives no further updates.

ProcessHackerPortable.Title: Renamed and superseded by System Informer
ProcessHackerPortable.Notice: Process Hacker has been renamed to System Informer and is now fully open-source. System Informer is the recommended replacement and is offered here.

qBittorrentEnhancedPortable.Title: Third-party qBittorrent fork with additional network behavior
qBittorrentEnhancedPortable.Notice: qBittorrent Enhanced is a third-party fork that adds extra network behavior, such as automatic peer banning. The added functionality and its independent maintenance are a trust consideration.

QupZillaPortable.Title: Discontinued Qt browser; superseded by Falkon, which is also discontinued
QupZillaPortable.Notice: QupZilla is discontinued and was succeeded by Falkon, which has also since been discontinued. It ships an outdated web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches.

SeaMonkeyPortable.Title: Old Gecko engine; receives web-security fixes slowly or not at all
SeaMonkeyPortable.Notice: This browser is built on an old Gecko engine and lags well behind Firefox, so it receives web-security fixes slowly or not at all. It is acceptable for local use, but outdated for general or sensitive web browsing.

SmartDeblurPortable.Title: Became commercial from version 2.0; this is the last freeware release
SmartDeblurPortable.Notice: Smart Deblur became a commercial product from version 2.0 onwards. The version available here (1.27) is the last release that was distributed as freeware.

SUMoPortable.Title: Discontinued KC Softwares utility; installers bundled offers
SUMoPortable.Notice: This KC Softwares utility is discontinued, and the vendor's installers historically bundled third-party offers. It is an unmaintained tool that checks for updates online.

SunbirdPortable.Title: Discontinued Mozilla calendar; ships an outdated, unpatched network engine
SunbirdPortable.Notice: Mozilla Sunbird is discontinued and no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

SupermiumPortable.Title: Unofficial, independently maintained browser fork
SupermiumPortable.Notice: This is an unofficial, independently maintained fork of a mainstream browser. It renders all web traffic and depends on a small team to keep pace with upstream security patches. No malicious behavior is documented; the consideration is trust and patch cadence.

SylpheedPortable.Title: Discontinued email client; ships an outdated, unpatched network engine
SylpheedPortable.Notice: Sylpheed is discontinued and no longer actively maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

SystemExplorerPortable.Title: Discontinued since ~2016
SystemExplorerPortable.Notice: System Explorer is discontinued and has not been maintained since approximately 2016.

SystemInformerPortable.Title: Legitimate tool that antivirus may flag (kernel driver abuse by malware)
SystemInformerPortable.Notice: This is a legitimate, advanced system and process tool. Its kernel driver is sometimes abused by malware to disable security software, so antivirus products may flag it as potentially unwanted. The tool itself is safe when obtained from a trusted source.

TeamViewerPortable.Title: Legitimate remote access; a common technical-support scam vector
TeamViewerPortable.Notice: TeamViewer is legitimate remote-access software, but it is among the most commonly abused tools in technical-support scams, and remote-access tools are a frequent intrusion vector — TeamViewer was the entry point in the 2017 CCleaner breach. A connection should only ever be granted to someone contacted directly and fully trusted.

UltraDefragPortable.Title: Open-source version discontinued; application continues commercially
UltraDefragPortable.Notice: The open-source version of UltraDefrag has been discontinued. The application continues under commercial licensing only.

uTorrentPortable.Title: History of bundled adware and a hidden cryptocurrency miner
uTorrentPortable.Notice: uTorrent's official installer bundled a cryptocurrency miner ('EpicScale') without clear consent in 2015 and has a long history of bundling adware. The torrent client itself is functional. Portable packaging excludes the official installer.

VirtualDubPortable.Title: Discontinued since ~2013; active fork VirtualDub2 is not offered here
VirtualDubPortable.Notice: VirtualDub is discontinued and no longer maintained since approximately 2013. The active fork VirtualDub2 exists as a replacement, though it is not offered here.

WackGetPortable.Title: Discontinued download manager
WackGetPortable.Notice: WackGet is discontinued and no longer maintained.

wxDownloadFastPortable.Title: Discontinued download manager
wxDownloadFastPortable.Notice: wxDownload Fast is discontinued and no longer maintained.

ZSoftUninstallerPortable.Title: Discontinued since ~2010
ZSoftUninstallerPortable.Notice: ZSoft Uninstaller is discontinued and has not been maintained since approximately 2010.
